reviews.title: Nice design

reviews.title: One of the best everyday mice I've used
reviews.text:
I have input devices for drawing on my tablet, and I have a gaming mouse, but the Logitech M305 is an excellent mouse for every day use, so it's the one I use the most.

The mouse itself has very basic features,
* scroll wheel (mild clicking feedback)with side-click options
* battery status light (green when fully charged, red when almost out)
* two buttons
* an off switch
* a nano USB transmitter than stores inside the mouse
* uses a single AA battery

It does come in jazzy designs, and I liked the yellow/gold one the best. The one thing I might have wanted to change on this mouse is the on/off switch. The best nano-USB mouse I've ever had would automatically turn off when you stored the transmitter inside. With this one, be sure to flip the switch to off as well before you put it in your bag, or it will be down there draining the battery as it moves around.

title: Rechargeable Wireless Mouse, 2.4G Dual Mode Slim Mute Silent Click Noiseless Portable Mobile Optical Mouse with USB Receiver Computer,3-Level Adjustable
description: LEGENDARY SOUND FROM A LEGENDARY PRODUCT The Klipsch ProMedia 2.1 was the first three-piece computer system to be THX certified, and its introduction singlehandedly raised the bar on what is defined as exceptional sound from CDs, MP3's and streaming radio.  Many have tried to copy its groundbreaking performance, but the ProMedia 2.1 still reigns supreme over anything near its price range. EXCLUSIVE HORN-LOADED TECHNOLOGY Klipsch Micro Tractrix horns make a major contribution to the Promedia 2.1's amazing proficiency.  Their highly efficient design reproduces more sound from every watt of power, controlling the dispersion of that sound and sending it straight to your ears for clarity and lifelike impact no other system can duplicate. CLEAN BASS OUTPUT AT ALL VOLUME LEVELS The two-way satellites' 3" midbass drivers blend perfectly with the Promedia 2.1's  solid, 6.5" side-firing, ported subwoofer for full bandwidth bass response you can actually feel.  With a separate subwoofer volume control, you can adjust for the best bass for what you're listening to. PERFORMANCE THAT CREATES FLEXIBILITY With its plug and play setup and convenient 3.5mm input, the Promedia 2.1 can double from anything from a TV sound enhancer to a 2.1 Home Theater system.  Its 200 watts of dynamic system power can fill even a larger room with sound.  The headphone jack allows for private listening when desired. Specifications Amplifier Power Peak Power: 200 Watts total syst...

reviews.title: These are great sounding computer speakers
reviews.text:
I have the Klipsch Pro Media Thx 2.1. These are great sounding computer speakers. They best the Bose Companion II which sound terrible in comparison. the Bose sound shallow and weak. These speakers have 3 1/2 inch long throw woofers for the midrange, and a real subwoofer for the lows. They are great sounding for what they are and the best computer speakers to my knowledge in the $200 or below genre.

However, they need to be tweaked. The subwoofer is slightly heavy (although an adjustment is on your speakers) and the highs are slightly bright like other Klipsch speakers, and the midrange is slightly recessed. Simply set your computer system from Windows at 70% output roughly, then pull up iTunes (if you use it) or another equalizer, and set the low bass from about 65K down about 3db below average, put the midrange from about 125K on up to about three 3DB higher than normal, then set the 1K at zero, and above that from 2K on up about 3db below zero. You can adjust that a little to make a curve

These speakers are great. I went with refurbished myself at a little over half price. Enjoy! They are great.

P.S. I am an audiophile who has a good ear and an extremely good stereo system. These do not equal that, but sound great for what they are and better than all others for $200 or less and close to the $400 plus systems.

reviews.title: Not natural sounding but that's how I like my computer speakers to sound
reviews.text: I'm not clear what the differences are between this model and the other identically labelled model from other sellers that sell for less money. You shouldn't pay more than $120-$135 for these from what I can tell after shopping around. I bought mine in "like-new" condition for $99 with Prime free shipping and I'm ecstatic about the sound quality. Subwoofer is boomy and the sat speakers are bright. Typical Klipsch sound. Not natural sounding but that's how I like my computer speakers to sound. I compared these to the Logitech at $105-$120. I own a pair of those too. I'm a fan of Logitech. Make that, I used to be a fan. Going to go with Klipsch if I ever have need for more speakers. These sound great. You won't be disappointed if you like the "Klipsch sound."

reviews.title: The satellite speakers are clear with a good amount of range and depth whether playing games or ...
reviews.text: First experience with Klipsch speakers and I am thoroughly impressed! Wasn't expecting the amount of impact from the subwoofer (my previous pc came with a factory 5.1 setup and fall flat compared to these). The satellite speakers are clear with a good amount of range and depth whether playing games or listening to music/watching videos. I was worried that a 2.1 setup would leave something to be desired vs a 5.1 setup, but I'm glad I made the jump!

reviews.title: Perfect
reviews.text: Perfect just as described

reviews.title: No better computer speaker out there
reviews.text:
There is simply no better set of computer speakers out there. The subwoofer rivals those home theater setups as well. I have this in my office connected to a Mac Mini which I often use for iTunes and watching movies, and recording to Garageband.

I have never cranked the volume 100%, it's incredibly loud at 65%. Bass is very smooth and powerful.

I actually needed speakers for an outdoor wedding and actually brought these to the event, no one complained about not being able to hear the ceremony even 20 rows back.

If you can't afford a home theatre system, consider this 2.1 for your living room as well. This will surprise you.

title: ROCCAT Kone XP Air – Wireless Customizable Ergonomic RGB Gaming Mouse, 19K DPI Optical Sensor, 100-hour Battery & Charging Dock, 29 Programmable Inputs & AIMO RGB Lighting, 4D Wheel – Black
description: The ROCCAT Kone XP Air Wireless Ergonomic RGB Gaming Mouse is designed to put the functionality of actions and macros at your fingertips. Perfected through years of refinement, this mouse is equipped with 10 dedicated buttons plus left, right, and vertical scroll wheel click functionality to expand into 29 programmable inputs, thanks to the Easy-Shift[+] duplicator button. Want to take this mouse on the go? Switch between 2.4GHz Stellar Wireless and Bluetooth for the ultimate in versatility. Additionally, the Kone XP Air’s Charging Dock blends beautifully into your setup and comes equipped with a USB passthrough on the front for the 2.4GHz wireless adapter. To further the immersive RGB experience, the translucent shell and Krystal 4D Wheel set the stage for the Kone XP AIR’s 5 lighting zones, creating stunning lighting effects. ROCCAT AIMO lighting technology produces vibrant RGB lighting displays right out of the box or syncs with other compatible AIMO products. The Kone XP Air packs a performance punch with ROCCAT’s Optical Titan Switches for lightspeed actuation and Owl-Eye 19K dpi optical sensor for precise movements. The heat-treated glides and a Phantom Flex charging cable to even further amplify your unrestricted gameplay, giving you the fastest, smoothest, and most precise experience possible. ERGONOMIC, MULTI-BUTTON DESIGN - Perfected through years of refinement, the beloved Kone ergonomic shape ensures comfort over long gaming sessions. Ten buttons plus 4D wheel expand into 29 programmable inputs thanks to the Easy-Shift[+] duplicator button. STELLAR WIRELESS + BLUETOOTH - Switch between gaming grade 2.4GHz STELLAR WIRELESS and Bluetooth for versatile use. Game all night with up to 100 hours of battery on a full charge. RAPID CHARGE DOCK - The stylish Rapid Charge Dock provides five hours of play time with just 10 minutes of charging. With 4 RGB lighting zones, the dock beautifully accents any desktop setup. Additionally, the 2.4GHz dongle can be plugged into the USB passthrough on the front. AIMO ILLUMINATION - The translucent shell and wheel set the stage for the Kone XP AIR’s five RGB zones, creating a stunning lighting experience. ROCCAT’s AIMO lighting technology produces vibrant RGB lighting displays right out of the box and can sync with other compatible AIMO-enabled products for an immersive lighting display. OWL-EYE OPTICAL SENSOR - Experience smooth, precise tracking thanks to ROCCAT’s Owl-Eye 19K DPI optical sensor. TITAN OPTICAL SWITCHES - ROCCAT’s Titan Optical Switches provide unprecedented speed, and incredible durability with a 100 million click life cycle offering a competitive edge game after game. PHANTOMFLEX CHARGING CABLE - Flexible and light, ROCCAT’s PhantomFlex USB-C charging cable virtually disappears during use to the point that you won't even know it's there. PTFE GLIDES - The Kone XP Air’s heat-treat

reviews.title: Super fun RGB lighting and great for Hell Let Loose
reviews.text: I wanted something with more buttons for Hell Let Loose than the Basilik V3. This mouse is ideal for those that need extra buttons for gaming as that is the primary reason you should consider it. The thumbrest is great as I think all gaming mice should have thumbrests since it’s so much better for your hand ergonomics. I have large hands and the mouse feels very good to hold and gives no fatigue. At first I though my thumb would hit the easy shift button but it didn’t so that’s another plus. I would say the left and right click buttons could be a bit wider and more ergonomic to say something like Basilik v3 which is where that mouse is a bit better but overall negligible. Of course the RGB is where the mouse really looks great, I love seeing so many fun colors move around and it looks really pretty to look at. So if you have a game you really like to play and are annoyed using your keyboard for some critical commands like in Hell Let Loose (map, team chat, squad chat, leadership chat) this is a very pretty, well designed convenient button placement mouse, I would recommend it.

reviews.title: Very nice gaming mouse
reviews.text:
This mouse is very ergonomic, has many buttons, and the software is good. I play many different games and need button profiles for them. The software is easy to use, and it is easy to associate a game/program with a profile for auto-switching when it is run.

I seem to use the buttons/profiles of gaming mice much more than some people. When I read mouse reviews, they mostly talk about the weight of it and other silly things that only first person shooter people care about. I care much more about the number/placement of extra buttons and the software used to configure the mouse.

I have several nice mice from other brands, brought down by HORRIBLE software. Logitech and Corsair make nice mice, but their software is a deal breaker. Roccat's mouse software is WONDERFUL.

I also like that the mouse has optical switches, which will last longer.

Only downsides: Not AS MANY buttons as my Roccat LEADR (started registering the dreaded double-click fault) and the software only supports 5 profiles at once. If you play more than 5 games that need a profile, you have to do the export/import dance. But this is a trivial fault.

The LEDs are a little loud, but I'm not a big LED/RGB lighting person anyway, so you can't fault the mouse. And you can turn them off (though I tend to set the colors to identify the game, so I can make sure I'm in the correct profile.)

Wonderful gaming mice that can be used for many different genres: MMO, FPS, etc.

reviews.title: upgrade from the KONE AIMO
reviews.text: I had to upgrade my previous mouse the KONE AIMO. It has a known issue with the left click which was repaired for a period of time then started acting up. I didn't lose hope in the brand however, because I absolutely love everything else about the AIMO! I play World of Warcraft and use sooo many key binds, so having a mouse like this is very helpful to my gameplay. The Kone XP is awesome. Lighter than the AIMO, similar button style but just feels smoother. Highly recommend it.

reviews.title: Dazzling and customizable...
reviews.text: that's what makes this such a fun mouse for me to use. It matches the keyboard in my home office and while I don't do PC gaming as much as I do on my laptop, this makes for a fun user experience no matter its purpose and it is highly attractive, affordable and easy to use.

reviews.title: Great battery life
reviews.text: I'm still not fully decided about how I feel about this mouse. But, I can talk about what I know so far. The packaging I got the mouse and was really nice, really simple, but easy to open. The mouse was easy to set up, and the RGB looks pretty cool. There is a really cool DPI changer function that makes gaming much easier, however, I can't recommend this mouse for gaming because the ergonomics are pretty trash. I have to cramp my hand like a claw over the top of the mouse and after long periods of use it does start hurting. The surface is also pretty slippery, so I found that my hand slips on the mouse all the time. The tracking however, is great! And I would have to say that biggest plus is the battery life. I got this about 2 weeks ago, charged it once, and it is yet to die! I think the features of this mouse are great, but I would not recommend it. Purely because of the ergonomics, if you are going to use this mouse often, then you probably should go if something more ergonomic.

reviews.title: Basic but functions well
reviews.text: I ordered a ‘LED Wireless Mouse, Uiosmuph G12 Slim Rechargeable Wireless Silent Mouse, 2.4G Portable USB Optical Wireless Computer Mice with USB Receiver and Type C Adapter (Silver)’ for review.  For the price point, this is a basic mouse.  The rotating light strip is a cool looking feature.  It is sleek in design.  It connected well with the dongle to my Surface Pro.  It glides well over a mouse pad.  It is rechargeable, which I prefer to battery operated.  The click of this mouse is quiet. The dongle slides into the bottom and of course it has already been lost.  I prefer the dongle to be stored in a closed compartment on a mouse.  It does not require recharging for several days of moderate use and seems to charge quickly.  Overall, for basic use, this mouse works well.

reviews.title: Work great
reviews.text:
The factory feet on my mouse were leaving dark spots on my light colored desk at work.
These fit great and have noticeably lower friction than the worn factory pads. The adhesive is strong but repositionable if you don't get it right first time.
When the old pads are removed, they can split into separate layers, but you should be able to get down to the bare plastic if you take care to grab the lowest layer of clear adhesive.

reviews.title: It's a mouse, it works.
reviews.text:
When purchasing a bluetooth mouse, or any bluetooth device, an important factor is how well it connects and how well it STAYS connected. After all this time, the industry still can’t make up its mind about how bluetooth works:
- Microsoft with their mouse and Sony with their controller that pair without the need of a code (which Android’s bluetooth stack doesn’t support).
- Mice and keyboards that will not reconnect after it wakes from sleep mode.
- To this day, bluetooth devices still can’t wake a computer from suspend state.
So when I get a simple bluetooth mouse for a good price, and it actually works as expected, I was happy.

For my review, I’m comparing the Bornd mouse to the [[ASIN:B006428K7I Motorola 89519N Bluetooth Mouse]] which I got in a set that included the [[ASIN:B004L9MBH6 Motorola Wireless Keyboard for Motorola ATRIX and XOOM (Motorola Retail Packaging)]].

With the Motorola mouse, its frequency somehow conflicts with the Motorola keyboard, resulting in sticky keys, as in “I’m having a good daaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaay”. Conversely, the keyboard interferes with the mouse where the computer would actually lose connection with the mouse for a few seconds after typing, or lose connection completely.

What I liked about the Bornd:
- Pairing was simple using the button at the bottom of the mouse.
- Operating distance is about 5 feet, but this may be because of my computer’s bluetooth antenna
- Takes a single AA battery which lasts over a month of moderate use (2 hours per day)
- Mouse movement is good. No need to adjust mouse speed in the control panel.
- Accuracy is good. Editing images using Pixlr.com, I’m able to select precisely the parts of the image I need.

What gave me trouble with the Bornd:
- When you first put your hand on this mouse, you will not feel that it is very well built. The plastic is completely smooth and the mouse wheel seems to rattle when you shake it. I got used to it after a few minutes, though.
- The mouse reconnects with the computer most of the time, but sometimes it was necessary to turn the mouse off and on again after the computer reboots or if the computer was out of range for an extended period of time.
- Lately, I’ve been experiencing double-clicks when I click on the left mouse button once. I’m usually able to fix this by getting angry and slamming the mouse down a couple of times. This may indicate a build quality issue with this mouse.

In my specific case, the Bournd did not interfere with my Motorola keyboard at all and it seems the sticky key occurs only when the batteries on the keyboard runs low.

reviews.title: I really do "love it".
reviews.text: I am using this device with an Asus Transformer Book T100TA touch screen notebook I recently purchased.  The device was recognized and connected to the notebook with no unusual delays or difficulties.  it works well with the PC as I recline in my easy-chair, using small motions upon the arm of the chair and allowing me to navigate through email and web browsing and to play games.  It is a nice compliment to the available on-screen gesture controls of the computer, especially for a die-hard convert from Windows XP and Windows 7 operating systems.

title: SHARKOON Drakonia Gaming Mouse - Black
description: The Drakonia Black is the follow-up model to our popular Green Drakonia. Like before, the Drakonia Black has eleven buttons and a 4-way scroll wheel, with each being freely programmable through the included configuration software to perform various different functions. An ADNS-9800 laser sensor from Avago operates under the gray dragon scales casing, with the current DPI setting in use indicated by a constantly illuminating LED. The SoftSkin surface of the Drakonia Black offers a soft grip and prevents perspiration build-up, ensuring the mouse fits perfectly in the hand even during heated battles. Likewise, the ergonomic shape of the Drakonia Black is designed for stability and control: its outwardly curved underside offers more grip during very small and precise movements as well as a high-level of comfort during long gaming sessions. Therefore the Drakonia Black is aimed especially at FPS Snipers and RTS/RPG gamers - the type of players who typically launch themselves into long battles and epic adventures. In the underside of the Drakonia Black you will find six metal weights comprising the Weight Tuning System, allowing the fighting weight of the mouse to be adjusted at any time. Unleash your inner Dragon – with the Drakonia Black!

gold-plated usb connector

Insert the dongle into the USB port of your device, and open the mouse switch (at the bottom of the mouse). 3. Allow a few seconds for installing, no action needed, just wait. Then you can use it. Note: If your computer is Type-C port, please insert the USB-C to USB receiver into the Type-c port of the device first, then insert the USB receiver into the USB-C to USB receiver. Turn on the power switch and the mouse will work. Tips: The mouse cursor will move slowly or stop moving when the mouse is low battery. Please charge it on time. The blue light beside the scroll wheel will be on while charging, and then goes off when the mouse is fully charged.

title: Logitech POP Mouse, Wireless Mouse with Customizable Emojis, SilentTouch Technology, Precision/Speed Scroll, Compact Design, Bluetooth, Multi-Device, OS Compatible - Mist
description: Logitech POP Mouse - Silent Wireless Mouse Let personality POP on your deskspace and beyond with POP Mouse, your whisper-quiet, wireless mouse with fresh customization features. Take your self-expression further and download Logitech Software to customize the top button. Give it your own twist by assigning it to send your favorite emoji or another shortcut - such as Snip Screen, Mic Mute or Voice-to-Text. POP Mouse fits comfortably in your hand and slips easily into your pocket, handbag or computer case. SilentTouch technology makes every click whisper-quiet. The portable mouse's SmartWheel stays in sync with you, easily shifting from high-precision to speed scroll mode. Pair POP Mouse with up to 3 different devices at once, and use the Easy-Switch button to toggle between them. Alternatively, activate FLOW and glide between screens as you work, transferring text, files and images with a simple copy and paste. Connect the cordless mouse to your desktop, tablet or phone* using Bluetooth or a Logi Bolt USB receiver which you can purchase separately. With a classic Logitech promise of durability, POP Mouse is your companion for the long haul, with up to 24-month battery life** and auto-sleep power-saving technology that helps it last. Product details: Number of buttons: 4 Top button customizable with Emoji Software Sensor technology: Logitech High Precision Optical Tracking Battery type: 1 x AA battery (included) Battery life: Up to 24 months** Connection type: Bluetooth 5.1 Compatible with Logi Bolt USB receiver Wireless range: 32.81 ft (10 m) Compatible with Windows 10 or later, macOS 10.15 or later, iPadOS, Chrome OS and Android *Windows, macOS, Chrome OS, Android, iPadOS **Battery life may vary based on use and computing conditions.

Bold, comfy, quiet: A compact and comfy wireless mouse with up to 24-month battery life that fits snugly in your palm, handbag and computer case, with SilentTouch Technology for whisper-quiet working
The emoji button: Press the colorful mouse's magic middle button to open a menu of emojis to use in your chats, or give it your own shortcut using Logitech Software (Windows and macOS only)
Shift gears on the fly: As if reading your mind, POP Mouse’s SmartWheel shifts from high-precision tracking to speed scroll mode, helping you whizz through long documents with one confident flick
FLOW between worlds: Connect to up to 3 devices on Windows, Mac, Chrome OS or Android using Bluetooth and glide between screens using the Easy-Switch button or FLOW
Aesthetic pair: Match your POP Mouse with the fun and bouncy typing experience of POP Keys wireless mechanical keyboard, with customizable emoji keycaps and cutting-edge media keys

Plastic parts in POP Mouse include certified post consumer recycled plastic (Blast: 46%; Daydream, Heartbreaker and Mist: 29%; Cosmos: 27%*). *Excludes printed wiring assembly (PWA) plastics, and packaging.
Certified carbon neutral: the carbon impact of POP Mouse has been reduced to zero.

reviews.title: Horrible Connection
reviews.text: I’ve tried this mouse with multiple devices now and it disconnects and reconnects every five seconds. It’s basically impossible to use.

reviews.title: Quiet and super fast Bluetooth setup - no Emoji function with iPadOS
reviews.text: Within a minute of taking this out of the box, I had it paired with my iPad. It was seriously so easy to just pair and go, and I love how it can be paired with three different things (just push a little button on the bottom of the mouse to switch between computers/iPads). It feels okay in my hand - not the highest quality I’ve seen (plastic is very basic and it is pretty lightweight as well), but it doesn’t feel cheap. The color is so fun, which is the main reason I got it. Also it is very quiet and doesn’t have any loud clicking. The one thing to mention is that the circle button under the scrolling wheel doesn’t have any functionality with an iPad. Nothing was happening when I kept clicking it to try and use the emoji shortcut, so after heading over to Logitech’s website i saw “*Emojis and software are currently supported on Windows® and macOS only.” So maybe iPadOS will eventually support this capability, but as of now it is useless. Overall I still like the mouse a lot for it’s ease of use, quietness, and color.

* Multi device with a click of button underneath the mouse. You can save up to 3 devices.
* Battery is included and is rated for 2 yrs. Wow, we'll see.
* Emoji dedicated center button for chats.
* Weighs 2.9 ounces.

THE BAD:
------------
* No information in box whatsoever about the emoji software. You'll need to go to Logitech's website and do some digging. I didn't bother since my little girl won't be chatting.
* Bluetooth only. No mentioning in the manual whether or not it works with their unifying receiver.

I got this mouse because my little girl loves pink, and the red coral color here truly pops! She does basic homework stuff on the pc. She's not at that chatting age yet, so I don't really care or tested the emoji feature. Logitech didn't make it easy for you to test it out anyway, so I didn't bother. I'll go 4-Stars overall.

reviews.title: great trustworthy brand, ergonomically comfortable with hours of use.
reviews.text: we have used logitech for so many of our technology needs over the decades and we love the new wireless mouse, which was super easy to connect.  the mouse can connect up to 3 appliances including tablets.  works for both android and apple.  very effortless to use and silent. the emoji button is not what we were looking for, but a plus.  Not being a Y gen - lol, it took me a while to figure how to work the emoji button.  again, not a key aspect for us.  Curious to test that the battery will last for 1 year as claimed, but easy to take off the 'color' plate and replace the AA battery.  very happy with the color.  fun and ergonomically comfortable.

reviews.title: I like the mouse
reviews.text: It is a piece of cotton candy fluff in my hands. I can move quickly over my laptop screen and edit easily. It paired immediately too. For those who like social media, you get an emoji button too. You just push on the wheel and hit the right part of the mouse top. I do not like social media anymore. But others do all the time.  They will love the ease of this mouse.

title: Kensington Pro Fit Mid-Size Wireless Mouse, Sapphire Blue (K72421AM)
description:
Product Description
Kensington Pro Fit Mid-Size Wireless Mouse Sapphire Blue (K72421AM)
From the Manufacturer
The Kensington Pro Fit Mid-Size Wireless Mouse provides users with clutter-free productivity for a desktop PC. And it complements mobile laptop usage for travel with a storable nano receiver. The ergonomic, right-handed design with a high-definition optical sensor offers precise and responsive control. The Pro Fit Mid-Size Wireless Mouse is built to last with a limited lifetime warranty.
features: Compact size for travel convenience clutter-free wireless technology and storable Nano receiver
Ergonomic right-handed design has scroll wheel with middle button and pointer speed quick-select
2.4 GHz wireless for long range with minimal interference
High-definition optical sensor (1750 DPI) for responsive control
Extended battery life up to 12 months
Compatible with Windows 10, Windows 8, Windows 7, Mac OS X and later
Compact size for travel convenience clutter-free wireless technology and storable Nano receiver
Note : In case of Wireless mouse, the USB receiver will be provided inside or along with the mouse
Ergonomic right-handed design has scroll wheel with middle button and pointer speed quick-select
2.4 GHz wireless for long range with minimal interference
Compatible devices: All iOS

reviews.title: Pretty good
reviews.text:
It's the best wireless mouse I have used so far, and the price was reasonable. It does have a tendency to skip around if the battery starts to get low of if it's not really close to my laptop. It does work, it fits nicely in my hand and it feels sturdy. I've had others that felt as if they would shatter if just dropped on the carpet.

Comfort - Great
Price - Fair
Battery life - Okay
Sturdiness - Good
Good product overall

reviews.title: A good one, but could be better
reviews.text:
Pros:
- light weight, convenient
- works
- batteries last a long time
- price is low
- on/off button

Cons
- pointer is a bit too sensitive
- compared to wired mouse, precision can be improved
- the little stick is easily lost

reviews.title: good mouse, great blue color
reviews.text: Very good mouse, and I ove the dark blue color, and it goes great with the new Blue Swirl mousepad that I also got from Amazon!

reviews.title: Nice mid sized mouse
reviews.text:
So far so good.
Easy to use and setup.
Fits my hand comfortably.
Just got it, will update if anything goes wrong.

reviews.title: ** Use tips ** Nice mouse....
reviews.text:
Nice mouse.... slightly large, and I have large hands. If you have small hands, I would purchase a size down.

It is simple to set up, works well (so far), and I like the wireless feature.

Tips:

My other mouse had a place to store the USB unit. This one also has a storage slot, which makes it convenient. Otherwise, you can leave the USB connector in the laptop (If it fits in your case with the device inserted...)

Be sure to turn off the device when not in use to conserve your batteries. (Sliding switch on bottom)

This mouse is Windows compatible. I run Windows 7.... works fine.

If you have a trackpad that drives you crazy, these devices are great!

Enjoy!

title: Logitech Wireless Mouse M305 (Victorian Wallpaper) (910-002459)
description: The Logitech Wireless Mouse M305 offers exceptional comfort and cordless convenience with its ergonomically designed soft rubber grip and the Plug-and-Forget Nano-receiver.
features: Ergonomic design with soft rubber grip gives you exceptional comfort
Plug-and-forget Nano receiver is so small it can stay in your laptop
Logitech advanced 2.4 GHz wireless provides a powerful, reliable connection to your PC or Mac
Intelligent battery management delivers months of use and added convenience
High-definition optical tracking (1000 dpi) enables responsive and smooth cursor control

reviews.title: Mouse
reviews.text: I really like the Logitech Wireless Mouse M305. It was simple to hook up and I like the size. I like the fact it has a turn off switch underneath so I can extend the battery life. I use it with my desktop.

reviews.title: My favorite mouse
reviews.text: My favorite mouse. I have several of these, with different themes. They are reliable and just the right size for my adult female hands. Most adult mice are at least a little clumsy and over-sized, while chidren's mice are too small. Also this model comes in awesome designs, although this model has been STUPIDLY discontinued.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: Like small size.  Use with laptop I travel with and takes up less room.. Works great

reviews.title: Battery lasts forever!
reviews.text: Very good, and battery seems to last forever due to the auto-shutoff feature.  Also the design hasn't worn off one bit, which is nice.

reviews.title: Great MOUSE
reviews.text: Mouse works great. I prefer the M305 over the New one, because I feel "like I have more control with this model over the New faster replacement model.
